title: chaenactis glabriuscula var. gracilenta
intro: Chaenactis glabriuscula var. gracilenta, also known as the Island Pygmyweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chaenactis glabriuscula var. gracilenta include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
